factors.name: Fed Policy Expectations
factors.summary: Kevin Warsh (new Fed Chair implied context) and hawkish ECB member Schnabel both signaling rates may stay higher for longer; Warsh seen as hawkish on inflation trajectory. No imminent rate cuts priced in.

factors.name: Energy / Geopolitical Shock
factors.summary: US-Iran strikes over weekend resumed, then ceasefire talks resumed for Tuesday in Qatar. Oil jumped modestly then unwound; Strait of Hormuz risk remains elevated but not acute. Short-term relief bid but fragile.

factors.name: Real Yields & Duration Risk
factors.summary: Higher real rates (via hawkish Fed rhetoric) compress long-duration growth multiples. Tech mega-caps (NDX core holdings) particularly vulnerable to real yield steepening; 10Y Treasury likely to remain sticky.

factors.name: Sovereign De-Dollarization Trend
factors.summary: News reports $29T in sovereign capital pivoting away from dollar, toward gold and private assets; Bolivia abandoning 15-year peg signals FX instability. Structural dollar weakness potential but distant catalyst.

factors.name: Leverage Concerns in Equities
factors.summary: Bloomberg reports leverage fueling US stock rally is now a growing source of unease; risk-off or volatility spike could force deleveraging and hurt momentum stocks (NDX-heavy).

factors.name: Mega-Cap Tech Earnings / AI Capex Narrative
factors.summary: Apple WWDC 2026 shows 'material positive reset' of AI strategy per headline; hyperscaler capex appetite remains strong (Nvidia, cloud providers). No near-term earnings surprise catalyst visible in news window.

factors.name: China Industrial Demand & Margin Pressure
factors.summary: China industrial profits +21% YoY in May but growth slowing; auto maker profits down 19.8% despite export strength = acute margin compression. Negative for semiconductor / supply chain demand outlook.

factors.name: Data Releases This Week
factors.summary: US NFP, ISM Mfg PMI, CPI flash data due early week; no surprises yet. RBA minutes Tuesday could reignite EM FX volatility. ECB inflation data also watched—no hawkish shock yet but Schnabel warning suggests tightening bias.

factors.name: Crypto Correlation & Risk Sentiment
factors.summary: Bitcoin fell below $60k (weekend), signaling capitulation but also risk-off mood. BTC-NQ correlation elevated; weak crypto tone suggests defensive posture favors lower multiple growth trades.

factors.name: Technical Price Action
factors.summary: NAS100 down -0.75% in 4h and -0.58% in 24h—modest downside, not panicked yet. Intraday weakness into close Sunday, consistent with profit-taking post-weekend geopolitical relief bid unwind.

screening.narrative: NAS100 is caught between hawkish Fed messaging (Warsh, Schnabel) that keeps real yields elevated and supported, versus short-term relief from US-Iran ceasefire talks resuming. The index is down modestly on the session but not breaking key support; sentiment is cautious given leverage concerns and China growth headwinds. The dominant story remains that rate expectations are sticky-high, compressing long-duration growth multiples, while the geopolitical relief was priced in Friday and has partially unwound. This is a classic Phase 2 chop: move didn't extend far but buyers are not aggressive ahead of major data (NFP/CPI early week).
catalysts_ahead: US NFP (June, Friday) — major risk event
US ISM Mfg PMI (likely Tuesday/Wednesday) — Fed signal
ECB flash CPI (likely Tuesday) — Schnabel hawkish pressure
RBA Minutes (Tuesday) — EM currency volatility trigger
China NBS Mfg PMI (Tuesday) — growth signal
US-Iran Doha talks (Tuesday) — geopolitical de-escalation check
screening.risk_warning: If US-Iran talks collapse and oil spikes >10%, or if NFP surprise hawkish (e.g. >250k), NDX could break 28,650 support and test 28,300. Conversely, if Fed guidance softens or inflation data surprises dovish (CPI <2.5% YoY), real yields could compress and unlock a 1-2% rally. Leverage unwind risk is real on any volatility shock >2% down day.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in early phase 2; avoid chasing lower here. Wait for retest of 29,200–29,300 resistance to short, or hold and buy any dip to 28,900 support only on material dovish data surprise (CPI <2.5% or Fed pivot signal). NFP Friday is the key catalyst—do not size into event risk.

factors.name: AI capex and systemic risk warnings
factors.summary: BIS warned that AI investment surge poses systemic risk due to 'enormous debt and highly leveraged nonbank structures that can rapidly unwind.' This echoes dot-com/railway mania concerns and directly challenges the capex narrative underpinning mega-cap valuations.

factors.name: Excessive data center power/heat stress
factors.summary: Multiple reports flagging severe weather, grid strain, and insurance/repair cost risks for AI data centers. This introduces operational and margin pressure on hyperscaler capex ROI assumptions, undermining forward guidance credibility.

factors.name: Geopolitical relief (US-Iran ceasefire)
factors.summary: US and Iran agreed to halt attacks and resume Doha talks Tuesday; oil pared gains and fell below $70. Lower energy risk typically supports growth equities and reduces inflation hedging demand, benefiting duration-sensitive mega-caps.

factors.name: Crypto ETF outflows and crypto weakness
factors.summary: Spot Bitcoin ETFs on track for worst month on record ($4B+ outflows); BTC holding below $60k. Crypto weakness historically signals risk-off repositioning and reduced appetite for high-beta, long-duration tech.

factors.name: Near-term data calendar intensity
factors.summary: Week includes US NFP (Friday), ISM Manufacturing PMI, multiple inflation prints (EZ Flash CPI, Swiss CPI, French HICP). Holiday-shortened week (US closes early Thursday, Friday). Volatility likely concentrated into these releases.

factors.name: China stimulus (PBOC easing signals)
factors.summary: PBOC set overnight reverse repo rate at 1.25% (below forecasts), signaling de facto rate cut. China's industrial profits up 21% in May but domestic demand lags. Mixed signal: easing tone positive for risk, but weak demand growth raises recession tail risk.

factors.name: Semiconductor positive catalysts
factors.summary: Samsung and SK Hynix $518B chip fab investment, South Korea $650B AI/chip mega-plan, Baidu AI chip unit (Kunlunxin) targeting $50B Hong Kong IPO. Supply chain demand signals remain robust despite capex quality concerns.

factors.name: Tech regulation/antitrust headwinds
factors.summary: Australia expanding regulator power over Big Tech; China blacklisting Japanese tech entities (Mitsubishi, Komatsu, Fujitsu units). Regulatory pressure mounting on global tech conglomerates with NAS100 heavy exposure.

factors.name: Dollar stability, real yields elevated
factors.summary: DXY flat at 101.35; USD remains bid despite Hormuz relief. Real yields (TIPS) still elevated, which caps multiple expansion for long-duration Nasdaq growth stocks. No fresh Fed dovish signal in news window.

factors.name: Recent price action (4h -0.75%, 24h -0.58%)
factors.summary: Index down modestly overnight despite geopolitical relief. Suggests sellers held on Iran ceasefire and AI concerns are overriding near-term safety bid. Move modest but directionally weak.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq100 is caught between conflicting forces: geopolitical relief (US-Iran ceasefire) and oil decline support risk appetite, while BIS systemic AI capex warnings, data center stress concerns, and record Bitcoin ETF outflows signal underlying risk-off repositioning in long-duration tech. Semiconductor capex announcements (Samsung/SK Hynix/Baidu) provide a positive counterweight, but their credibility is being tested by questions about hyperscaler ROI and the leverage funding them. With a data-heavy week ahead (NFP, ISM, multi-region CPI) and real yields still elevated, the index faces competing narratives until inflation/Fed expectations clarify. Current -0.75% 4h decline suggests intraday sellers are cautious despite headline relief.
catalysts_ahead: US NFP (Friday) — Goldman expects 130k headline with 40k World Cup distortion, implying 90k underlying (soft signal for September cut if realized)
US ISM Manufacturing PMI (Monday/Tue) — manufacturing weakness would support lower rates narrative
EZ Flash CPI (Tuesday) — ECB board member Schnabel called for further hikes despite Hormuz relief; hot inflation could reinforce hawkish tone
RBA Minutes (Tuesday) — Australian policy stance amid global uncertainty
PBOC/China Manufacturing PMI (Tuesday) — domestic demand weakness vs. easing bias
US Dallas Fed Manufacturing (Monday) — early June regional activity read
UK GDP Final (Tuesday) — Q1 growth revision
Mega-cap earnings announcements (dates not specified in window, but Apple, Microsoft, Google, Nvidia commentary on capex ROI critical to Nasdaq narrative)
screening.risk_warning: Geopolitical escalation (US-Iran talks collapse) could trigger an oil spike and flight-to-safety bid, reversing bearish crypto/AI capex narrative. Conversely, surprise hawkish CPI or Fed communication would pressure duration multiples further. Mega-cap earnings disappointment on guidance would amplify AI capex skepticism and trigger 2-3% selloff. Failure to hold 29000 support would signal deeper pullback toward 28700-28500.
screening.entry_guidance: Phase 2 / move modest and inconclusive. Avoid chasing downside; wait for clearer setup at 29000 support or hold for pullback/retest before committing. Data risk skews toward volatility into NFP Friday. Consider small longs only if 29250 resistance breaks with volume, or add to shorts on rollover through 28900.
